message:
fix(startup): pass resolved DB path to reconstruct closure, centralize doctor subcommand stdout suppression

Startup self-heal path resolution:
- run_reconstruct closure signature changed from FnMut() to
  FnMut(&Path) so the reconstruct path receives the resolved database
  path explicitly via resolve_mailbox_activity_sqlite_path()
- Both Repair and Reconstruct branches now delegate through
  run_startup_doctor_subcommand_quietly() instead of calling the closure
  directly, centralizing stdout suppression logic

Test rewrite:
- Old integration test (startup_database_self_heal_suppresses_doctor_
  stdout_output) replaced with focused unit test
  (startup_doctor_subcommand_quietly_installs_capture_for_doctor_output)
- New test verifies StdioCapture is installed during execution and
  released after, using stdio_capture_lock guard for thread safety

Minor fixes:
- Removed stray extra closing brace in CLI lib
- In write_startup_integrity_cache_atomic, replaced continue with {}
  on AlreadyExists error arm to avoid unnecessary explicit continue
  in the retry loop

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
